articles.title: 5 Short Daily Piano Routines for Absolute Beginners
articles.body:
Starting piano can feel overwhelming, but short, focused routines build skills quickly.

In this article you'll learn five simple daily practice structures that each take 10–15 minutes. We'll cover: warming up your fingers with easy five-finger patterns, building a sense of pulse with hands-together clapping, and practicing your first simple melodies without getting stuck.

Each routine includes a suggested timer setting, a sample exercise, and one way to make it easier or harder so you can adjust it over the first few weeks. By the end of the month, you’ll have a predictable routine that makes sitting down at the piano feel natural instead of stressful.
articles.excerpt: Build a 10–15 minute daily piano habit with five simple beginner-friendly practice routines.

articles.title: How to Practice Scales on Piano Without Getting Bored
articles.body:
Scales are one of the fastest ways to improve your piano technique, but only if you practice them with intention.

In this guide we’ll walk through a 3-step beginner approach: (1) learning the basic C major scale pattern with correct fingering, (2) adding a simple rhythm pattern to keep your brain engaged, and (3) using a "focus of the day" (tone, evenness, or relaxation) so every repetition has a purpose.

You’ll also get three mini scale games you can play in under 5 minutes, plus a printable checklist to track progress over your first four weeks.
articles.excerpt: Turn beginner piano scales into a focused, game-like practice that actually feels rewarding.

articles.title: Left Hand Made Easy: First Chord Patterns for Beginner Piano
articles.body:
Many new pianists feel confident reading the right hand, but the left hand feels clumsy and slow.

This article introduces three beginner-friendly chord patterns you can use in hundreds of songs: whole-note chords, broken chords, and the "boom–chick" pattern. You’ll learn which fingers to use, how to switch chords smoothly, and how to coordinate with simple right-hand melodies.

We’ll finish with a 15-minute practice plan that rotates these patterns through common progressions like C–F–G and A minor–F–C–G so your left hand starts to feel strong and reliable.
articles.excerpt: Strengthen your beginner piano left hand with three simple chord patterns you can use in real songs.

articles.title: 3 Common Beginner Piano Mistakes (and Easy Fixes)
articles.body:
Most beginner piano frustrations come from just a few small habits that are easy to fix once you spot them.

We’ll look at: (1) collapsing finger joints, (2) hunched shoulders and bench height, and (3) racing through pieces without a steady pulse. For each issue, you’ll get one simple test to see if it applies to you and one 2-minute exercise to start correcting it today.

These adjustments don’t require any special equipment—just awareness and consistency—and they can dramatically change how comfortable and confident you feel at the keys.
articles.excerpt: Identify and fix three tiny beginner habits that quietly hold your piano playing back.

articles.title: Your First 7 Days on Guitar: A Simple Practice Roadmap
articles.body:
If you’ve just picked up a guitar, the first week sets the tone for your progress.

This roadmap gives you a day-by-day plan covering tuning, holding the guitar comfortably, your first two chords, and a basic strumming pattern. Each day includes a 10-minute routine and a quick self-check so you know when you’re ready to move on.

By day seven, you’ll be able to switch between two chords in time with a metronome and strum along to a simple song at a slow tempo.
articles.excerpt: A clear, day-by-day plan for your very first week of guitar practice.

articles.title: Pop Vocal Warm-Ups You Can Do in 5 Minutes
articles.body:
Short, consistent vocal warm-ups keep your voice healthy and flexible.

We’ll cover three quick pop-focused warm-up sequences: lip trills for breath flow, gentle sirens to connect chest and head voice, and a simple 1–3–5–8 scale pattern on neutral syllables. Each warm-up includes recommended starting notes for different voice types.

Use these before practice, rehearsal, or gigs to feel more supported and confident when you sing.
articles.excerpt: A 5-minute warm-up routine to keep your pop singing voice flexible and healthy.

articles.title: New Online Lesson Studio Setup for 2026
articles.body:
The studio has a new online lesson setup for 2026, including upgraded cameras, studio lighting, and high-quality audio interfaces.

This article walks through what’s changed on the technical side and how it benefits your lessons: clearer camera angles for hands, real-time backing tracks with low latency, and better screen-sharing for sheet music.

You’ll also find tips to optimize your own home setup so you can get the most out of online lessons, even with a basic laptop and headphones.
articles.excerpt: Take a tour of the upgraded online lesson studio and how it improves your experience.

articles.title: How Live Jazz Changed the Mood of an Outdoor Wedding
articles.body:
When Emma and Leo planned their outdoor wedding, they pictured a relaxed, candlelit evening—but weren’t sure how live music would fit.

In this story, we follow the process of choosing a jazz trio, planning the ceremony and cocktail-hour playlists, and adapting to last-minute weather changes. You’ll see how small decisions—like using a softer trio texture during speeches—kept the focus on the couple while still giving guests a memorable atmosphere.

At the end, we include a sample set list and timeline you can adapt for your own wedding.
articles.excerpt: A behind-the-scenes look at planning live jazz music for an intimate outdoor wedding.

articles.title: Weekend Drumming: Practice Ideas When You Only Have 30 Minutes
articles.body:
If your drum practice only fits on weekends, it’s still possible to progress steadily.

This article offers a 30-minute structure you can repeat every Saturday or Sunday: 10 minutes of hands-only pad work, 10 minutes of coordination patterns between kick, snare, and hi-hat, and 10 minutes playing along with a song.

You’ll also get three beginner-friendly track suggestions at different tempos so you can apply your grooves right away.
articles.excerpt: A focused 30-minute weekend routine to keep your beginner drumming moving forward.

articles.title: Making Ukulele Practice Fun for Kids 8–12
articles.body:
Kids are more likely to practice ukulele when it feels like play, not homework.

We’ll share five simple game ideas you can use at home: chord bingo, tempo challenges, practice dice, call-and-response strumming, and a weekly "mini concert" for family members.

Each game includes a quick setup guide and suggestions for adapting it to different attention spans and skill levels.
articles.excerpt: Turn kids’ ukulele practice into a series of quick, playful games they’ll ask to repeat.

instructors.name: Emma Collins
instructors.bio: Emma specializes in helping adult and teen beginners start guitar with modern songs and solid technique. She focuses on clear practice plans and friendly accountability.

instructors.name: Sophia Rivera
instructors.bio: Sophia is a pop-focused vocal coach who helps singers build healthy technique while keeping a contemporary sound. She offers structured recurring lesson plans for long-term growth.

instructors.name: Lena Hart
instructors.bio: Lena introduces beginner violinists to solid posture and tone with a calm, encouraging approach. She offers flexible weekend scheduling for busy families.

instructors.name: Mike Johnson
instructors.bio: Mike loves helping beginner drummers lock in their first solid groove, with a focus on coordination and playing along to favorite songs.

instructors.name: Amy Chen
instructors.bio: Amy specializes in adult beginners returning to piano, blending reading skills with playing by ear and chord knowledge.

instructors.name: Luca Martinez
instructors.bio: Luca works with intermediate and advanced pianists on jazz harmony, improvisation, and comping skills.

instructors.name: Chris Patel
instructors.bio: Chris helps new singers build pitch accuracy and confidence with current pop and R&B songs.

instructors.name: Nora Williams
instructors.bio: Nora works primarily with kids and pre-teens on ukulele and beginner guitar, focusing on fun, song-based learning.

instructors.name: Aaron Blake
instructors.bio: Aaron is a multi-instrumentalist who helps beginners explore guitar, piano, or drums to see which instrument fits best.

instructors.name: Olga Petrova
instructors.bio: Olga prepares advanced violin students for auditions, competitions, and conservatory entrance exams.

instructors.name: Daniel Kim
instructors.bio: Daniel helps guitarists explore jazz harmony and improvisation, from first chord extensions to full soloing.

group_classes.name: Kids Ukulele Jam (Ages 8–12) – Saturday Mornings
group_classes.description: A fun, song-based ukulele group for kids ages 8–12. Students learn chords, strumming patterns, and play-along songs in a supportive small group (4–8 students). No prior experience required; loaner ukuleles available for the first few weeks.

group_classes.name: Spring Piano Explorers (Kids 8–12) – Sunday Session
group_classes.description: A beginner-friendly group piano class for kids 8–12. We cover basic keyboard geography, simple melodies, and first chords using a mix of games and ensemble pieces.

group_classes.name: Teen Pop Voice Lab – Thursday Evenings
group_classes.description: A small-group vocal lab for teens focused on pop repertoire, mic technique, and performance confidence. Includes group warm-ups and individual coaching moments.

group_classes.name: Adult Beginner Guitar Circle – Monday Nights
group_classes.description: A relaxed group for adult beginners to learn essential chords, strumming patterns, and their first full songs. Perfect for those starting from scratch or returning after a long break.

group_classes.name: Saturday Drum Grooves (Kids 8–12)
group_classes.description: Beginner drum class for kids focusing on basic grooves, coordination, and playing along with tracks. Practice pads provided; full kit rotations during class.

group_classes.name: Sunday Starter Strings – Beginner Violin (Kids 8–12)
group_classes.description: Group violin class introducing posture, bow hold, and simple ensemble pieces. Ideal as a supplement to school orchestra or private lessons.

group_classes.name: Family Ukulele Night – All Ages
group_classes.description: A light-hearted ukulele class designed for families to learn and play together. Chord sheets are tailored so kids and adults can participate at their own level.

group_classes.name: Adult Piano Basics – Weeknight Online Group
group_classes.description: A live online group class for adults learning piano from home. We cover reading basics, simple chords, and practice strategies for busy schedules.

group_classes.name: Kids Pop Choir – Saturday Session
group_classes.description: Small pop choir for kids 8–12 featuring contemporary songs, basic harmony, and movement. Great for building confidence and stage presence.

group_classes.name: Teen Songwriting Workshop – Online
group_classes.description: Weekly online workshop for teens to write original songs, get feedback, and learn basic recording skills. Open to singers and instrumentalists on any instrument.
